summary: Full e2e testing of Cartera de Créditos completed. Backend: 23/23 pytest cases passed covering config, clients CRUD + validation, credits creation + preview, pagar cuota, mora preview, cobranzas report, backup export, and referential-integrity deletes. Frontend: all critical UI flows work end-to-end (dashboard KPIs, client creation with inline DNI/telefono/nombre validation, edit client, credit creation with correct preview S/240 x 6, payment + receipt, receipt close, cobranzas report with 1 cobro visible + CSV enabled, config persistence across reload including brand-name reactive update, backup JSON download, credit + client deletion). Mobile responsive at 390x844: hamburger button visible, sidebar hidden by default (offscreen), opens with 'open' class on click, closes on backdrop click and on nav item selection. No blocking bugs found.

issues: Table columns overflow horizontally requiring horizontal scroll inside .table-wrap — acceptable UX pattern but consider adding a visual scroll cue

critical_code_review_comments: server.py get_config_doc: uses `{"_id": 0}` correctly to exclude Mongo _id.
server.py mora_preview line 329 uses `datetime.now()` (naive) while fechaVencimiento is parsed as naive — consistent, OK but consider using date-only comparisons everywhere for clarity.
App.js ShowReceiptFromCredit uses useEffect with empty deps and eslint-disable — works but consider passing setReceipt/closeModal via useCallback to satisfy exhaustive-deps rule.

PayModal initial state reads q?.capital.toFixed(2) — could crash if q is undefined during transition; the null-guard afterwards (`if (!cr || !q) return null`) runs after useState which is fine because optional chaining is used. OK.
backend export/import: import uses upsert on same id — good for merge, no dedup issues.
No auth on any endpoint — expected for this MVP but should be noted for prod hardening.
seed_data_creation: Tests self-create + self-cleanup TEST_ prefixed data. Confirmed DB is clean after run (0 clients, 0 credits, config reset to defaults).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_cartera_creditos.py. IMPORTANT: pytest.ini forces `-n 2 --dist loadscope` for xdist — this module uses a shared module-scoped `state` fixture that only works if the whole file runs on one worker. Run with `pytest -n 0 backend/tests/test_cartera_creditos.py` to force serial execution when combining files, or keep the module isolated so loadscope assigns it to a single worker (works with the current single-file run). All 23 tests passed in 1.86s.
